steps.title: Identify two clear points on the line of best fit
steps.content:
Read the graph carefully to find points where the line passes through grid intersections or clear locations.

The line crosses the y-axis at approximately $(0, 12)$. Moving to the right along the line, at $x = 10$, the line passes through approximately $(10, 1)$.

steps.title: Calculate slope using the rise-over-run formula
steps.content:
Apply the slope formula with the two points $(0, 12)$ and $(10, 1)$:

$$m = \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1} = \frac{1 - 12}{10 - 0} = \frac{-11}{10} = -1.1$$

The slope is negative because the line descends from left to right. That matches choice B — B is correct.
